strategy: Inspect the most recent error traceback or stderr output.
Locate the file and line causing the failure.
Write a minimal patch that fixes the root cause.
Add or update a regression test that reproduces the failure.
Run the test suite to confirm the fix.
Profile or inspect the slow / noisy code path.
Identify a low-risk refactor or caching opportunity.
Apply the change and keep the public API stable.
Run existing tests and a targeted benchmark.
Understand the feature request in the current codebase context.
Design the smallest vertical slice that demonstrates value.
Add the new behavior behind a clear API or CLI surface.
Write tests that exercise the happy path and edge cases.
Update README / USER.md if user-facing behavior changed.
